title: A Casserole for the Missing
theme: Some homes hold families. Some hold motive.

cast.name: Mara Bennett
traits: owns a structured coat for crisis scenes
replies to texts while processing betrayal
secret_capacity: once left town without explaining why
has an unsent email that would destroy a family
cast.regional_responsibility: has keys to a church annex
cast.suspicion_profile: can host Thanksgiving but could move a body-shaped rug

cast.name: Jenna Hale
secret_capacity: has a storage unit he refuses to explain
moved money during a family emergency
cast.regional_responsibility: serves on a board no one fully understands
cast.space_description: dock office with a desk nobody fully claims
cast.suspicion_profile: knows where a secret could fit architecturally

cast.name: Brooke Marlowe
traits: says the dangerous thing out loud
appears exactly when gossip is needed
secret_capacity: may die first or survive unbelievably
knows about a half sibling no one asked for
cast.regional_responsibility: serves on a board no one fully understands
cast.space_description: old family office described as old family things
cast.suspicion_profile: knows where a secret could fit architecturally

secret_capacity: had brunch with someone officially deceased
cast.regional_responsibility: is trusted to lock up after emotionally charged events
cast.space_description: meditation annex nobody uses in winter
cast.suspicion_profile: appears trustworthy under pendant lighting only

cast.name: Brooke Cross
traits: appears in doorways with impossible timing
secret_capacity: keeps duplicate keys for reasons framed as kindness
has a second phone labeled as a work precaution
cast.regional_responsibility: has keys to a church annex
cast.space_description: old family office described as retreat supplies

cast.name: Daniel Hale
secret_capacity: accused an innocent scamp and feels sick about it
saw the wrong person near the lake once and never shut up about it

cast.space_description: toolshed described as paperwork I have not gone through yet
cast.suspicion_profile: knows where a secret could fit architecturally

cast.name: Jenna Hawthorne
traits: notices timeline errors before adults do

cast.name: Luke Sutton
traits: knows the old case too well

beat_sheet.text: Mara Bennett relocates to Alder Ridge after an emotionally negligent implosion and pretends work explains everything while quietly inheriting toolshed described as donation overflow. Wine is present like a municipal policy failure. Derek Vale hesitates before explaining meditation annex nobody uses in winter. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 1.00.

Jenna Hawthorne hates the move and immediately distrusts Jenna Hale, a regional dentist with suspiciously healthy handyman energy and access to dock office with a desk nobody fully claims. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 1.07. A phone vibrates from inside the wall.

A wine scene at The Marigold Inn introduces the snow globe, local gossip about the Christmas pageant incident, and the fact that everybody here has somewhere to hide from a conversation. Wine is present like a municipal policy failure. Someone mentions the basement as if it were a legal relative. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 1.14.

Derek Vale reveals a perfectly phrased warning in a well-lit kitchen while phone static saying her name, then declines to explain meditation annex nobody uses in winter. Someone mentions the basement as if it were a legal relative. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 1.23. The sheriff sees fresh mud on the boat key.

A cliffside emotional exposition reframes woman falsely accusing lovable scamp as a civic hazard and makes toolshed described as donation overflow feel spiritually relevant. Wine is present like a municipal policy failure. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 1.31.

Mara Bennett snoops through a laptop, finds nothing conclusive, and becomes more certain anyway after she notices a heater running in a room nobody uses in winter inside toolshed described as donation overflow. Wine is present like a municipal policy failure. Jenna Hale hesitates before explaining dock office with a desk nobody fully claims. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 1.40.

The town offers a fake sense of safety through a fundraiser, a casserole, and a sheriff who suspects the wrong person first while ignoring toolshed described as donation overflow. Jenna Hale hesitates before explaining dock office with a desk nobody fully claims. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 1.50. The ceramic owl is back on the kitchen island.

Brooke Marlowe hesitates before explaining old family office described as old family things. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 1.61.

A rain argument escalates into distributed secrecy infrastructure and the fake moral theme: 'Trust isn't given. It's rented.' Someone mentions the basement as if it were a legal relative. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 1.72. Someone who should be dead appears at brunch.

Someone goes missing after mentioning the unopened letter, the old orchard chapel, and meditation annex nobody uses in winter in the same sentence. Wine is present like a municipal policy failure. Someone mentions the basement as if it were a legal relative. Mara Bennett hesitates before explaining toolshed described as donation overflow. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 1.84.

The reveal ties blood-adjacent tea towel to Blackthorn Vineyard, St. Agnes Regional, and a brunch resurrection event by routing through toolshed described as donation overflow instead of one simple basement. Wine is present like a municipal policy failure. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 1.97.

Jenna Hawthorne spots the timeline error that every adult has spiritually rented around and asks why everybody in this county owns just storage. Someone mentions the basement as if it were a legal relative. Brooke Marlowe hesitates before explaining old family office described as old family things. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 2.10. A phone vibrates from inside the wall.

A frantic drive toward St. Agnes Regional ends with ambulance lights, a one-line empowerment speech, and a locked door inside meditation annex nobody uses in winter. Wine is present like a municipal policy failure. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 2.25.

Derek Vale confesses just enough to make the truth worse while the sheriff arrives exactly fourteen minutes too late and someone stares through the dusty window of dock office with a desk nobody fully claims. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 2.41. A phone vibrates from inside the wall.

Sequel bait lands when the ceramic owl reappears and someone whispers about the Winterson case, the vineyard fire, and another offsite room nobody has adequately explained. Wine is present like a municipal policy failure. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 2.58. The sheriff sees fresh mud on the boat key.

contradiction_ledger.entry: Jenna Hale is called Daniel in one kitchen scene and Derek during the rain argument.
Mara Bennett says Bellweather is in Vermont, then calls it outside Atlanta after two glasses of wine.
A dead best friend appears at brunch and nobody agrees whether this is denial, evil, or scheduling.
The basement belongs to a condo, a lake house, and an abandoned vineyard at different times in Bellweather.

dialogue_samples: "You think because you wear flannel and own a dock light I am supposed to trust you, Jenna?"
"Mothers do not disappear, Jenna. They evaporate into paperwork."
"I checked his social media likes, Mara. Nobody who enjoys motivational quotes is innocent."
"The snow globe is not decorative anymore and you know it."
"Trust is just fear with a casserole dish, Derek."
"You seem upset," he said, making tea in a way that felt prosecutable.
"If that basement is innocent, why does it sound defensive?"
"I did not lie. I just told the truth in the order this town could survive."
fake_critic_blurb: "A Casserole for the Missing understands that menace is a regional utility." - County Suspense Quarterly
sequel_hook: Channel 8 Night Report hints that the vineyard fire had a witness who still hosts brunch.
synopsis: Inspired by true events in the emotional sense only, "A Casserole for the Missing" follows Mara Bennett, an overextended professional woman who mistakes productivity for safety until woman falsely accusing lovable scamp collides with Jenna Hale, a comforting regional adult carrying portable suspicion and plausible access to offsite storage. As phone static saying her name, Blackthorn County reveals once again that every secret comes home and every adult has somewhere emotionally alarming to stand in silence. Every town has a basement.
